title: About SAMMs | Complex Needs Clinical Program Australia
description: SAMMs is an award-winning, evidence-based, neuro-informed complex needs clinical program by Creating New Pathways, supporting long-term unemployed participants across employment, health, justice and community systems.
body: HomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchEmergencyHomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchEmergencyHomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchAbout UsThe clinical model built for those systems overlook.SAMMs bridges the gap when surface-level engagement and referral loops fail, providing immediate, evidence-based clinical intervention for profound and overlapping complex needsWhat is SAMMsA clinical framework that turns complexity into capacity.SAMMs (Safety, Awareness, Motivation & Management) is an evidence-based, neuro-informed framework that stabilises crisis, strengthens capacity, and integrates learning into lasting behavioural change.Developed through more than 30,000 hours of frontline practice and lived experience, SAMMs translates neuroscience into practical recovery across employment, justice, health, and community systems.Regulate→Build CapacityIntegrateStep 1Regulate To stabilise the nervous system and create safety before any behavioural change begins.SAMMs clinicians begin by calming the physiological stress response - reducing overwhelm, threat sensitivity, and reactivity.​ Participants learn emotional regulation, grounding, and awareness techniques that prepare the brain for capacity-building and employment readiness.The People Behind SAMMsGet In TouchLed by CEO Sharon Mamo, the program is grounded in lived experience and refined through more than 15 years of frontline work and over 30,000 appointments in the spaces most systems avoid. In 2025, Sharon and Montana Nicoloutsopoulos, Director of Clinical Programs and an AASW Social Worker of the Year nominee, researched, reviewed, and manualised the model into a fully developed, evidence-based framework.SAMMs bridges critical access gaps with immediate, evidence-based intervention, not surface-level engagement or endless referral loops. title: Australia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service for Employment Services - My Framer Site
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: HomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchEmergencyHomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchEmergencyHomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchAdvocacy & Policy NewsAustralia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service for Employment Services4 minutesbyMontana NicoloutsopoulosMedia releaseEmployment services reformComplex needsAustralia’s first self-funded Complex Needs Clinical Service designed specifically for Employment Services participants opens tomorrow in Sunshine, marking a major milestone for long-term unemployed people navigating the increasingly complex Centrelink system.The SAMMs Complex Needs Clinic is a first-of-its-kind clinical service built for people facing entrenched, high-risk and long-term barriers including mental health challenges, addiction, criminal justice involvement, disability, chronic illness, trauma and severe social instability.Located at 1/5 Devonshire Road, Sunshine, the clinic launches in the same community where Sharon Mamo, CEO of Creating New Pathways and founder of the SAMMs model, once faced the very barriers she now specialises in addressing. For Ms Mamo, this opening is both a full-circle moment and a turning point in how complex unemployment is understood and supported in Victoria.Built by lived experience, proven through clinical designMs Mamo and her daughter, Montana Nicoloutsopoulos, a Mental Health Social Worker and 2025 Australian Social Worker of the Year nominee, self-funded the research, development and build of the SAMMs model. Their mission was to create a clinical service that acts quickly, intelligently and compassionately for people repeatedly failed by overstretched systems and reduced to tick-box compliance.These are individuals who often feel isolated, misunderstood and unable to advocate for themselves. Many struggle to meet constantly shifting Centrelink requirements without the support, regulatory capacity or clinical scaffoldin

title: The Partnerships That Built SAMMs - My Framer Site
description: Creating New Pathways delivers the SAMMs framework to support complex needs participants with trauma-informed, neuro-informed care embedded within employment services and beyond.
body: HomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchEmergencyHomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchEmergencyHomeAboutResourcesEmergencyContact UsTerms of ServiceGet In TouchAdvocacy & Policy NewsThe Partnerships That Built SAMMs4 minutesbySharon MamoClinical innovationEmployment services reformComplex needsWhy SAMMs ExistsSAMMs exists because people inside the system saw the cracks and had the courage to ask for something better. We were invited to step in where traditional counselling, activation and short-term interventions had already failed the people they were meant to help.The Early WISE Employment CollaborationIn the early years, WISE Employment engaged SAMMs CEO, Sharon Mamo, and Director, Montana Nicoloustopoulos, to assess and respond to a clear service gap within their ex-offender and complex-needs caseloads, an area both organisations recognised as critical to reform.At WISE’s request, Sharon and Montana provided extensive clinical consultation, program redevelopment, and workforce guidance to help their sites respond safely and effectively to participants facing entrenched psychosocial and neurological barriers.Building the FrameworkOver more than two years, Creating New Pathways self-funded and delivered thousands of professional hours to the sector, including clinical program design, case reviews, staff consultation, and system improvement.During this time, SAMMs developed a comprehensive model of care that integrated neuroscience, trauma-informed practice, and behavioural regulation principles into employment servicing.These activities informed the creation of the SAMMs Clinical Framework model owned and governed by Creating New Pathways, and now implemented nationally.What the Early Results DemonstratedThe early collaboration demonstrated the value of embedding clinicians within employment systems, turning engagement into stability, and crisis into capacity.Ongoing Employment PartnersWe also acknowledge our on
